name: Portes – circular por Cabezón de Liébana y Porcieda
region_intro: Mountain biking around Potes offers access to the diverse terrain of the Liébana region and the Picos de Europa National Park. The area is characterized by towering limestone peaks, deep valleys, and verdant forests, providing varied elevation changes. Rivers like the Deva and Quiviesa flow through the landscape, adding to the natural features. This environment creates a challenging and rewarding experience for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Potes The most popular mountain bike trail is Collado Pelea y Minas de Andara – circular al Macizo Oriental de Picos de Europa desde Portes, a 41.8 miles (67.3 km) difficult trail that takes 7 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route explores the eastern massif of the Picos de Europa, featuring significant elevation gains. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Portes – circular por Cabezón de Liébana y Porcieda, a difficult 15.6 miles (25.1 km) path. This circular route traverses the valleys of Liébana, offering varied scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Alcornocal de Tolibes – circular desde Potes, a 22.8 miles (36.7 km) trail leading through cork oak forests, often completed in about 4 hours 20 minutes. Mountain biking around Potes is defined by towering limestone peaks, lush valleys, and ancient forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from demanding climbs to scenic valley paths. The routes in Potes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Potes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Potes?
A: Potes offers an extensive network of mountain bike trails, with over 30 routes documented on komoot. These trails cater to various skill levels, providing diverse options for exploring the Liébana region and the Picos de Europa National Park.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Potes?
A: The months of July and August are particularly popular for mountain biking in Potes due to favorable weather conditions. However, the region's diverse ecosystems mean that spring and early autumn can also offer pleasant riding experiences with vibrant landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails near Potes?
A: While many trails around Potes are rated as difficult, there are moderate options that families with some biking experience might enjoy. It's always recommended to check the specific route details for elevation gain and technical sections to ensure it suits your family's ability. Currently, there are 12 moderate trails available.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on mountain bike trails around Potes?
A: The terrain around Potes is highly varied and often challenging. You can expect dramatic landscapes featuring towering limestone peaks, lush valleys, and ancient forests. Many trails include significant elevation gains and can feature technical elements like rock gardens, rock faces, and wallrides, especially on more difficult routes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Potes?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169366964/portes-circular-por-cabezon-de-liebana-y-porcieda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portes – circular por Cabezón de Liébana y Porcieda&lt;/a&gt; route is a difficult 25.1 km loop that traverses the valleys of Liébana, offering varied scenery. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000724987/alcornocal-de-tolibes-circular-desde-potes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcornocal de Tolibes – circular desde Potes&lt;/a&gt;, a 36.7 km trail through cork oak forests.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or landmarks I can see while mountain biking near Potes?
A: The region offers breathtaking views. You can ride towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3601813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Viorna Cross&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/176220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jano Peak&lt;/a&gt; for extraordinary 360-degree panoramas of the high mountains and valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5345646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de las Arredondas Trail&lt;/a&gt; also offers stunning views. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3474751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Saint Toribio of Liébana&lt;/a&gt; is a significant historical site nearby.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that lead to or pass by natural features like forests or rivers?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000724987/alcornocal-de-tolibes-circular-desde-potes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcornocal de Tolibes – circular desde Potes&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, takes you through ancient cork oak forests. The Deva and Quiviesa rivers flow through the heart of Potes, and many trails will offer views of these waterways and the lush valleys they carve.

Q: What is the difficulty level of mountain bike trails around Potes?
A: Mountain bike trails around Potes are predominantly challenging. Out of over 30 routes, 18 are rated as difficult, and 12 are moderate. There are currently no easy trails listed, indicating that most routes require a good to very good fitness level and some technical skill.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Potes?
A: The mountain bike trails in Potes are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Many riders praise the varied terrain, the dramatic scenery of the Picos de Europa, and the rewarding challenge of the climbs and descents.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that offer high-altitude exploration?
A: Yes, Potes serves as a gateway to the Picos de Europa National Park, which is characterized by significant elevation changes. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169839644/collado-pelea-y-minas-de-andara-circular-al-macizo-oriental-de-picos-de-europa-desde-portes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Pelea y Minas de Andara – circular al Macizo Oriental de Picos de Europa desde Portes&lt;/a&gt; explore the eastern massif, featuring substantial elevation gains and offering high-altitude experiences. The cable car at Fuente Dé, just 23 km from Potes, also provides quick access to high-altitude starting points for further exploration.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer views of the Picos de Europa?
A: Many trails around Potes provide spectacular views of the Picos de Europa. The region is defined by its proximity to this national park, and routes often ascend to viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/176220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jano Peak&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3601813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Viorna Cross&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive vistas of the jagged limestone peaks and deep valleys.
tips: A forest of cork oaks that can be covered almost entirely by following the large number of paths that there are through it and having at your hands these curious trees from which the cork is extracted. A very quiet and picturesque place with beautiful views of the surrounding environment.

This abandoned town has a special charm. Surrounded by cork oaks and with some buildings in ruins, the only thing that remains standing and still in good condition is the hermitage that is worth visiting if you feel like it.

This beautiful church dates from the end of the 12th century, although it is believed to have existed since the year 930 according to records from the Piasca Monastery, very important in the area. It underwent subsequent reconstructions around the 15th century and unfortunately it is not always open to the public.
It is located on top of a small hill at the foot of Pico Pumar. The hermitage dates from the year 990, every year on August 6 there is a big party and the holy image is carried around.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa María de Piasca
Hermitage of the Virgin of Valmayor
